what_it_does: Stores YAML frontmatter metadata for a channel attachment named att-d74be923, declaring it belongs to the dragnet-development channel, has attachment_type board_items, and targets board://item-479ff4dd with an added timestamp.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter; there is no body content.
Fields present: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
The target uses a board:// URI scheme pointing to item-479ff4dd.
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset.
standouts: The target is a board:// URI, indicating a link to a board item rather than a file or URL.
The attachment_type is board_items, suggesting this attachment is categorized as board-related content.
conventions: Attachment id uses the att- prefix.
ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit UTC offset.
notes: This is a lightweight metadata stub that records when an attachment was linked to a specific board item in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or payload does this attachment actually carry?
What is the semantic meaning of attachment_type board_items?
Is board://item-479ff4dd resolvable within the system?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose appears to be recording an attachment-to-board-item association, which matches the content.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to persist a timestamped, typed relationship between a channel attachment and a board item for later retrieval or audit.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme as a target is a distinct linking convention.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed association record connecting an attachment to a board item, which a fused system could use to reconstruct attachment lineage and board references.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment metadata stub in the dragnet-development channel that links attachment att-d74be923 to board item item-479ff4dd as a board_items attachment added on 2026-06-24.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a channel attachment is associated with a specific board item at a known time.
